source.description: Six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the June 5, 2026 honest archive backfill marker-doodle lesson, reviewed before cropping.
source.notes: Prompt required one consistent marker magnifying glass doodle, no sticker or badge framing, no embedded labels, numbers, arrows, signatures, watermarks, or fake UI.

process_strategy: The sequence builds the lens circle, handle, thick rim, glass shine, small search spark marks, shadow, and marker fills before the final panel only thickens outlines and clarifies existing color.

final_elements.name: round lens
final_elements.description: A large circular lens tilted slightly on the page.

final_elements.name: short handle
final_elements.description: A simple handle attached diagonally to the lens.

final_elements.name: thick lens rim
final_elements.description: A bold ring around the lens.

final_elements.name: handle end cap
final_elements.description: A rounded end on the handle.

final_elements.name: glass shine streaks
final_elements.description: A few curved shine marks inside the lens.

final_elements.name: tiny search spark marks
final_elements.description: Small simple sparkle marks near the lens, without words.

final_elements.name: blue marker glass and gray shadow
final_elements.description: Pale blue marker fill in the lens and a small gray ground shadow.

frames.visible_job: Draws the round lens and short diagonal handle with simple marker construction.
Adds the thick lens rim and rounded handle end cap.
Adds curved shine streaks inside the already-drawn lens.
Adds tiny search spark marks near the lens and starts bolder keeper outlines.
Adds pale blue marker fill to the lens and a small gray ground shadow.
introduces: blue marker glass and gray shadow

final_step.visible_job: Thickens the existing black outlines, evens the blue marker glass fill, clarifies the shine streaks and spark marks, and strengthens the same shadow.

rejection_checks: No lens, handle, rim, shine, spark mark, shadow, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may ink, fill, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
